commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility tradeoff plus normal required-check resolution, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair.; Cleared: The diff only reduces Claude Code built-in tool availability in the Anthropic CLI backend and does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, permissions expansion, or supply-chain surfaces. (sha=5828313082bc2e27bbaa35634f537e78decb0061)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `--disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate` to the fresh and resumed Anthropic `claude-cli` default argv arrays.

PR surface: Source +4. Total +4 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main's fresh and resume Claude CLI argv do not deny these built-ins and the runner uses those arrays; the PR also supplies before/after terminal proof showing `claude -p` accepting `ScheduleWakeup` before and rejecting both tools after.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility tradeoff plus normal required-check resolution, not a narrow ClawSweeper repair.

target.title: fix(cli): disable ScheduleWakeup/CronCreate in --print claude runs
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `ScheduleWakeup` and `CronCreate` are Claude Code built-in tools that schedule deferred re-invocations managed by the persistent CLI runtime. In OpenClaw's one-shot `claude -p` runs the process exits at `end_turn`, so any wakeup or cron registered during the run has no host to fire into — it is silently lost. Symptom: a CLI session spawns a background sub-agent, calls `ScheduleWakeup` to poll for completion, ends the turn, and never picks up the result — work finishes unreviewed with `lastMessagePreview: NO_REPLY`.
- **Solution:** Append `--disallowedTools "ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate"` to both `args` and `resumeArgs` in the anthropic CLI backend so the model cannot invoke tools that don't survive the run mode.
- **What changed:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` — +4 lines in two static arrays.
- **What did NOT change:** Codex / Gemini backends, interactive TUI mode, Pi-backed sessions, `--allowedTools` surface, normalisation logic.

## Motivation

In a real OpenClaw deployment, an agent session running through `claude -p` called `ScheduleWakeup(270s)` to poll a background sub-agent, then ended the turn. The wakeup never fired — the session showed `status: done` / `lastMessagePreview: NO_REPLY` while the sub-agent's file changes sat unreviewed on a feature branch. The right pattern for background work in a `--print` session is `Monitor` on the output file, or a synchronous sub-agent. Disabling the tools at the argv level ensures the model cannot reach for an escape hatch that does not work in this mode.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration (cli-runner / anthropic claude-cli backend)

## Linked Issue/PR

Related #83552 (same class: `--print` CLI session harness gaps). Checked: this PR fixes a bug or regression.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `ScheduleWakeup` / `CronCreate` invoked inside a `claude -p` one-shot run schedule a deferred wakeup that has no host to fire into — the wakeup is silently dropped, the session ends with `NO_REPLY`, and the background sub-agent's output is never reviewed.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw v2026.5.18 stable (and v2026.5.20 patched) on WSL2 Ubuntu (Linux 6.6), Node 24.15, anthropic claude-cli backend, live gateway running locally and handling real `agent:main:*` webchat lanes. The patched build is the one currently driving the affected install.
- **Before evidence:** prior to this patch the spawned `claude -p` argv allowed every Claude Code built-in tool, so the model could and did call `ScheduleWakeup`. Captured production-side behaviour at that time: session record showed `lastMessagePreview: NO_REPLY` after the model called `ScheduleWakeup(270s)` and then `end_turn`; the gateway received no further turn for that lane until the user manually intervened; the background sub-agent's resulting file changes sat on a feature branch with no agent follow-up.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** built OpenClaw from the patched source and restarted the local gateway onto the new build. Every claude-cli session OpenClaw spawns now goes through this argv (live terminal output below, copied from `pgrep -af '^claude '` on the patched-build host):

```
claude -p --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user \
    --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* \
    --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate \
    --resume <session-id> \
    --permission-mode bypassPermissions --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path> \
    --plugin-dir <path> --effort medium --model opus \
    --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json \
    --permission-prompt-tool stdio --replay-user-messages
```

- **Evidence after fix:** copied live terminal output from the patched-build gateway — `pgrep -af '^claude '` against the running gateway captures redacted runtime logs of every active claude-cli child carrying the new flag:

```
98957 claude -p ... --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate ...
99368 claude -p ... --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate --resume <session-id> ...
```

`--disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate` is present on both fresh runs (`args`) and resumed runs (`resumeArgs`), exactly the two argv arrays the patch touches. When the model attempts to call either tool inside the patched run the Claude CLI returns a tool-not-allowed error and the agent falls back to `Monitor` on the sub-agent's output file or to a synchronous Bash invocation. No new `NO_REPLY` outcomes for this class have been observed since the patched build went live on the affected install.

- **Observed result after fix:** the model cannot reach either deferred-wakeup tool inside `claude -p`; tool-not-allowed errors route the model to the working alternatives (`Monitor` on the sub-agent transcript file, or synchronous Bash with a generous timeout); affected sessions stay coherent through the wait instead of ending in `NO_REPLY`. Live `claude` children running on the gateway carry the `--disallowedTools` flag continuously, confirming the static argv arrays in `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` are the authoritative source of the spawn argv.
- **What was not tested:** Codex / Gemini backends (no change; not re-exercised); Pi-backed orchestrator (Pi has its own wakeup dispatch and is unaffected); a downstream plugin that might re-allow `ScheduleWakeup` via its own argv injection (the cli-runner does not currently expose that seam).

## Root Cause

`ScheduleWakeup` / `CronCreate` are unconditionally surfaced by the Claude CLI regardless of run mode. OpenClaw's one-shot `-p` invocations exit at `end_turn`; no daemon remains to service the wakeup. The anthropic CLI backend restricted MCP tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`) via `--allowedTools` but did not restrict the Claude Code built-ins that cannot survive a `--print` exit. Pi harness has its own wakeup dispatch (correct); the CLI backend did not match the run mode's actual lifetime.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A — the failing scenario requires a live Claude Code binary executing a real one-shot run. The change is a two-entry append to two static string arrays; the live `pgrep` output in the proof section above is the directly observable result. The only meaningful test is an integration test against a real `claude -p` binary, which is outside the scope of this minimal surface-reduction fix.

- [x] Existing coverage already sufficient (argv content is not unit-tested today)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Models running inside OpenClaw's `claude -p` sessions can no longer call `ScheduleWakeup` or `CronCreate`. They receive a tool-not-allowed error and fall back to `Monitor` (for background sub-agent output) or synchronous Bash with a generous timeout.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
  [claude -p] -> model calls ScheduleWakeup(270s) -> process exits -> wakeup ghost -> NO_REPLY

After:
  [claude -p --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate] -> tool-not-allowed -> model uses Monitor or sync Bash
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions: No
- New secrets handling: No
- New network calls: No
- New exec surface: No (this is a surface *reduction* — two Claude Code built-in tools are blocked in `--print` mode)
- New data access: No

## Repro + Verification

- **Environment:** OS: Linux (WSL2 6.6); Runtime: Node 24.15, OpenClaw v2026.5.18; Model/provider: anthropic / claude-cli backend (`claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`); Channel: webchat / `agent:main:*` lane.
- **Steps:** open an OpenClaw CLI-backed session; have the model call `ScheduleWakeup`; observe — before the patch the call silently succeeds and the wakeup is lost, after the patch the call is rejected and the model recovers.
- **Expected:** the model receives a tool-not-allowed error and routes to `Monitor` or synchronous Bash.
- **Actual (after the patch):** `ScheduleWakeup` and `CronCreate` are rejected at the Claude Code built-in tool layer; the session stays coherent.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace logs / terminal capture (live `pgrep` output above showing the patched argv on the running gateway)
- [x] Surface reduction is directly observable in the spawn argv (no test layer required)

## Human Verification

Verified: the `--disallowedTools` flag is accepted by the `claude` binary on the affected install; `pnpm tsgo` is clean on the patched file; the `resumeArgs` path (session resume) carries the same flag as the fresh-run `args` path; normalisation functions do not strip the flag. Live `pgrep -af '^claude '` against the running gateway shows every spawned `claude` child carrying `--disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate`. NOT verified: downstream effects if a plugin explicitly re-allows these tools via its own argv injection (no such plugin path is exercised in this PR).

## Review Conversations

- [x] Will resolve bot review threads before merge
- [x] Will respond to maintainer review comments

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible. No config or migration. Silent success in `--print` mode was already broken (the wakeup never fired); an explicit error is strictly better.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** a model that relied on `ScheduleWakeup` inside a `--print` session now sees a tool error instead of the prior silent success.
  **Mitigation:** the prior silent success was already broken — the wakeup never fired. The explicit error lets the model branch to a working alternative (`Monitor` or synchronous Bash).
- **Risk:** future plugin authors might want to opt back into these tools for a specific deployment.
  **Mitigation:** the change lives in two static argv arrays in the anthropic CLI backend; reverting per-deployment is a small, well-scoped follow-up if maintainers ever decide to support it.
target.author: skywolfdreamer

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
